provider_approval_type: approved by the Office of the Attorney General (OAG)
applicable_to: commercial lodging establishments with 10 or more rooms
required_topics: Identification of human trafficking risk factors
Role of staff in reporting and responding to human trafficking incidents
Information on the National Human Trafficking Hotline and local law enforcement agencies
Understanding of commercial lodging's role in addressing sex trafficking
Information on human trafficking signage required

required_content: Identification of human trafficking risk factors
Role of staff in reporting and responding to human trafficking incidents

certificate_requirements.requirements: provide a certificate of completion to each employee

law_references: House Bill 390 in the 87th Regular Session
risks_or_constraints: Costs associated with training and signage may vary depending on the course and provider
Not required for smaller establishments, which could lead to inconsistent compliance across the industry
